core.when: When computing forward returns from price data
core.action: use shift(-period) to correctly align future prices with current factor values

consequence.description: Using shift(period) instead of shift(-period) introduces look-ahead bias, causing future information to be used in historical analysis and producing backtests that cannot be replicated in live trading

core.when: When simulating entry signals in backtesting
core.action: Process entry signals at current candle open price, not future candles

consequence.description: Entry signals using future price data create look-ahead bias, causing backtest results to be unrealistically profitable

core.when: When incrementing stock day data in MongoDB
core.action: query the last stored date from database and only fetch data after that date

consequence.description: Re-downloading existing data causes duplicate entries and incorrect backtest results due to overlapping timestamps

core.when: When implementing SMA or other TA-Lib functions to accept pandas Series input
core.action: Return pandas.Series with the same index preserved from the input Series

consequence.description: Breaking pandas index preservation causes downstream code to lose temporal alignment, leading to incorrect backtest results and misaligned signal generation

evidence_refs.summary: talib/__init__.py:67-69 extracts index from first pandas Series; lines 97-99 pass index to output Series constructor

core.when: When implementing order direction logic in backtesting
core.action: Use positive order amounts for buy (long) positions and negative amounts for sell (short) positions — maintain consistent sign convention throughout order processing and position calculation

consequence.description: Inverting the order sign convention causes buy orders to be interpreted as sells and vice versa, resulting in completely opposite trading behavior with catastrophic losses in backtest and live trading

core.action: introduce inherent 1-period lag through rolling window or shift operations

consequence.description: Signals generated without lag will exhibit look-ahead bias, causing live trading returns to fall far below backtested results because the strategy would have traded on information not yet available

evidence_refs.summary: src/finmarketpy/economics/techindicator.py:92-93(rolling mean inherently produces lagged values) and tests/test_techindicator.py:55(expected_df = data_df.shift()) shows indicator aligns with shifted p

src/finmarketpy/economics/techindicator.py:92-93(rolling mean inherently produces lagged values) and tests/test_techindicator.py:55(expected_df = data_df.shift()) shows indicator aligns with shifted p

core.when: When preparing historical data for backtesting
core.action: Verify startup candle count equals or exceeds longest indicator lookback period

consequence.description: Insufficient startup candles cause indicators to reference future data, introducing look-ahead bias that inflates backtest performance

core.when: When fetching market data from external data sources
core.action: Implement fallback mechanism when data fetch returns None

consequence.description: Network failures, API errors, or missing credentials cause fetch_market to return None; without fallback, the entire backtest fails without generating any results

evidence_refs.summary: finmarketpy_examples/tradingmodelfxtrend_example.py:139-144 shows explicit None check with CSV fallback

core.when: When computing rolling volatility and Sharpe ratio
core.action: annualize volatility using sqrt(APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR) where APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR equals 252

consequence.description: Annualized volatility and Sharpe ratio will be incorrectly scaled if using calendar days instead of trading days

core.when: When implementing volatility targeting with rolling window periods
core.action: account for NaN values in the first N periods before the rolling window completes

consequence.description: Volatility-adjusted leverage will be NaN for the initial periods equal to the rolling window (vol_periods), causing backtest P&L series to contain NaN values for warmup periods and potentially causing downstream calculation errors in portfolio aggregation

evidence_refs.summary: src/finmarketpy/backtest/backtestengine.py:2875-2876 explicitly sets lev_df[0:vol_periods] = np.nan to ignore elements before the vol window kicks in; rolling_volatility at line 2848 requires vol_peri

core.when: When preparing datasets for machine learning model training and validation
core.action: Split data temporally into TRAIN, VALID, TEST segments using date range boundaries — verify each TRAIN timestamps precede VALID, which precedes TEST

consequence.description: Random or incorrect temporal splitting introduces look-ahead bias where future information leaks into training data, causing backtest results to significantly overestimate live trading performance

core.when: When implementing Double Exponential Moving Average (DEMA) indicator
core.action: Calculate DEMA using the formula: DEMA = 2*EMA(period) - EMA(EMA(period), period). This specific coefficient structure achieves approximately half the lag of standard EMA. Do not simplify or re-derive the formula.

consequence.description: Incorrect DEMA formula variations produce different lag characteristics that alter signal timing. Short-term strategies relying on DEMA crossovers will generate signals at different prices, causing backtest results to diverge from expected short-term trading behavior.

core.when: When validating position state during order execution
core.action: validate position is finite during each order processing

consequence.description: Infinite or NaN positions cause arithmetic failures in size calculations and corrupt trade PnL records

core.when: When processing order records to build trade records
core.action: validate order size is greater than zero before creating trade records

consequence.description: Zero or negative order sizes in trade generation produce invalid trade records with corrupted size fields and incorrect PnL calculations

core.when: When implementing data ingestion to data filtering data flow
core.action: Maintain chronological order with no temporal gaps in OHLCV bar timestamps

consequence.description: Backtest equity curve exhibits look-ahead bias when non-chronological data causes indicator values to reference future prices, leading to live trading returns that fall far below backtested results
